Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd (EAM) was incorporated in Singapore on 2015-10-08.
From around 2015 to 2020-04-01, EAM presented itself as engaging in a business of physical nickel trading described as the Purported Nickel Trading.

From 2016-02-01 onwards, EAM and its staff sent emails and marketing materials to potential and existing investors describing a nickel trading model involving purchase of nickel from Poseidon Nickel Limited and sale to buyers such as China MinMetals Corporation and BNP-related entities.

From around 2015, investors entered into Letters of Agreement with EAM under which investors provided investment amounts for three-month terms stated to be used solely for investment in LME nickel products, with options to roll over or withdraw at maturity.
Under the Letters of Agreement, EAM typically stated that investors would receive at least 85% of their invested principal upon maturity of each three-month term.
From 2016-02-01 to 2021-03-01, EAM issued monthly spreadsheets to investors showing purported profit and loss and returns on their individual investments.
From 2016-02-01 to 2021-03-01, EAM circulated fact sheets, prospectuses and slide decks describing its nickel trading model and historical returns.

On 2016-10-06, an email was sent from EAM to an investor describing a nickel deal involving purchase from Poseidon Nickel Limited in 2016-02-01 and delivery to China MinMetals Corporation in 2016-04-01, and an aluminium deal involving Meikles EC and China MinMetals Corporation in mid-2016.

In 2016, EAM issued a prospectus prepared with input from its management which stated that EAM was licensed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore and categorised as a Registered Fund Management Company.
Between 2018-04-26 and 2021-02-10, funds were transferred from bank accounts of EAM and Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd to bank accounts bearing the names EAM Trading or Envy Asset Management Trading.
From 2016-01-01 onwards, EAM made payments to investors that exceeded their original investment principal, described to investors as referral fees or profits.
From 2016-01-01 onwards, EAM made payments to its directors and employees in the form of salaries, allowances, bonuses, commissions, profit sharing, directors' fees and other reimbursements.
From 2016-01-01 onwards, EAM transferred funds to entities related to the Envy group or to companies owned by Envy entities or Ng Yu Zhi.

From around 2020-02-01, promotional material circulated by the Envy group described Envysion entities as part of a wider Envy group and stated that Envysion Wealth Management Pte Ltd held a Capital Markets Services Licence from the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

In early 2021, during a webcast with investors, a video of a stock inspection at Steinweg Warehouse in Tuas was shown and described as a stock count of nickel briquettes that investors had invested in during late 2020.
From 2016-01-01 onwards, EAM operated an investment structure in which new investor funds were used to make payments to earlier investors, to staff, and to related entities.
From 2016-01-01 onwards, EAM did not operate a separate, documented revenue-generating business apart from the investment scheme described to investors.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a corporate entity operating a scheme utilizing new investor funds to meet outstanding obligations, without an underlying revenue-generating business, engages statutory avoidance mechanisms to recover distributed funds upon insolvency.
Party_Details.Rule: Under Singapore insolvency and company law, when a company becomes insolvent, transactions transferring company property—including outsized payments to investors, directors, employees, and related parties—may be set aside. Funds disbursed in furtherance of a fraudulent scheme are recoverable for the benefit of the creditors under statutory provisions concerning transactions at an undervalue, unfair preferences, and transactions to defraud creditors.
Party_Details.Application: Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd (EAM) received substantial funds from investors under Letters of Agreement, representing that the funds would be used for physical nickel trading. However, EAM did not operate a legitimate revenue-generating business; instead, it used incoming investor funds to make payments to earlier investors, directors, employees, and related entities. The company distributed prospectuses falsely claiming regulatory licensure and circulated altered inspection videos to sustain the inflow of funds. Upon its insolvency and subsequent liquidation, these disbursements lack valid commercial consideration.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: When a corporate entity operates a Ponzi scheme involving non-existent commodity trading, the transfer of company property to investors, directors, and related parties without legitimate commercial basis engages insolvency and avoidance provisions. The operation of an investment structure where new investor funds are used to pay earlier investors, absent a genuine revenue-generating business, constitutes fraudulent trading and an unlawful means conspiracy. Consequently, payments made under such a scheme are recoverable under statutory mechanisms for transactions at an undervalue, unfair preferences, and transactions to defraud creditors, alongside common law claims for unjust enrichment and knowing receipt to restore the insolvent estate.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Envy Management Holdings Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: Envy Management Holdings Pte Ltd (EMH) was incorporated in Singapore on 2020-06-18.
From 2020-06-18, Envy Management Holdings Pte Ltd was the sole shareholder of Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a holding company whose sole subsidiary engages in a fraudulent investment scheme falls within the scope of insolvency and recovery proceedings to trace and recover misappropriated group assets.
Party_Details.Rule: A holding company that owns a trading subsidiary may be subject to insolvency and avoidance provisions where corporate property is transferred or liabilities are incurred in the context of an overarching fraudulent investment scheme affecting the entire corporate group.
Party_Details.Application: Envy Management Holdings Pte Ltd functioned as the sole shareholder of Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd during the period when the subsidiary actively received investor funds under Receivables Purchase Agreements and unlawfully disbursed those funds. This shareholding structure forms the basis for tracing funds and assessing group-level financial obligations within the insolvency framework.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: A holding company that owns a trading subsidiary actively involved in an unlawful investment scheme serves as a conduit for group liabilities. The structural integration of the holding company into the broader corporate group engages insolvency and avoidance provisions, permitting the tracing of funds and the assessment of liability across the corporate structure to maximize asset recovery for defrauded creditors.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd (EGT) was incorporated in Singapore on 2019-12-10.
From around 2020-04-01, the business of the Purported Nickel Trading previously conducted by Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd was transferred to and conducted by Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.

From around 2020-04-01 to 2021-03-01, investors entered into Receivables Purchase Agreements with Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd to invest in receivables under Forward Contracts relating to nickel trades.

Under the Receivables Purchase Agreements, Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd typically stated that investors would receive at least 85% of their Sale Price or principal at maturity, with options to roll over or withdraw.
Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd represented to investors that BNP Paribas SA and Raffemet Pte Ltd were Forward Buyers under certain Forward Contracts.

From around 2020-05-01 to 2021-02-01, Envysion Wealth Management Pte Ltd and Envysion Commodity Strategy Fund invested approximately S$56,500,000 and US$15,400,000 into the Purported Nickel Trading via Receivables Purchase Agreements with Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.

In 2020-08-01, Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd purchased approximately 2969.26 metric tonnes of primary nickel briquettes from Raffemet Pte Ltd in a transaction later referred to as the Singapore Nickel Shipment.
Between 2020-08-01 and 2020-09-01, Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd resold the nickel from the Singapore Nickel Shipment back to Raffemet Pte Ltd.

On 2020-08-24, a physical inspection of nickel briquettes at Steinweg Warehouse in Tuas was conducted and recorded on video, and video links to this inspection were circulated to investors of Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd and related entities.

Between 2018-04-26 and 2021-02-10, Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd and Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd transferred a total of approximately S$416,575,000 and US$17,665,000 from their bank accounts to accounts bearing the names EAM Trading or Envy Asset Management Trading.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a successor trading entity that assumes control of a fraudulent investment scheme is entitled to recover disbursed funds under insolvency laws.
Party_Details.Rule: Where a trading company receives investor funds under contractual arrangements without engaging in underlying genuine commerce, and subsequently enters insolvency, the law dictates the recovery of those payments to satisfy the claims of defrauded creditors.
Party_Details.Application: Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd (EGT) took over the investment scheme from Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd in April 2020, entering into Receivables Purchase Agreements with investors. It engaged in only a single documented physical nickel shipment with Raffemet Pte Ltd while continuing to misappropriate massive sums of investor funds (transferring over S$416 million to associated accounts) to fulfill the scheme's outgoing obligations. Upon EGT's insolvency, its liquidators assert the right to claw back these funds.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: An entity acting as the primary operational vehicle for a fraudulent investment scheme incurs massive, illegitimate liabilities to its investors. When such an entity enters insolvency, the flow of funds through its accounts to various recipients is subject to strict scrutiny. Payments made from investor funds without corresponding legitimate commercial consideration are recoverable by the insolvent estate to satisfy the Minimum Net Principal owed to defrauded investors.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Bob Yap Cheng Ghee

Facts.Text: On 2021-08-16, Bob Yap Cheng Ghee was appointed as one of the liquidators of Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd, Envy Management Holdings Pte Ltd, and Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.
Bob Yap Cheng Ghee was appointed as one of the liquidators of the Envy Companies on 16 August 2021.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a court-appointed liquidator has the authority and evidentiary standing to pursue recovery actions against recipients of funds from an insolvent scheme.
Party_Details.Rule: Liquidators are empowered under the Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act (IRDA) to investigate company affairs, identify transactions that may be set aside (such as fraudulent trading or transactions at an undervalue), and pursue recovery actions for the benefit of creditors.
Party_Details.Application: As a joint liquidator appointed on 16 August 2021, Bob Yap Cheng Ghee collects and analyses data on investor inflows and outflows, identifying the absence of genuine trading activity. He calculates the Minimum Net Principal owed to investors to initiate recovery actions against parties who received funds from the insolvent Envy Companies.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Liquidators possess the statutory authority to investigate the financial affairs of an insolvent entity and pursue recovery actions against recipients of misappropriated company funds. By reconstructing the flow of funds and establishing the absence of legitimate trading, the liquidators establish the evidentiary foundation required to unwind voidable transactions and recover assets for the benefit of the defrauded creditors.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Tay Puay Cheng

Facts.Text: On 2021-08-16, Mr Tan Puay Cheng was appointed as one of the liquidators of the Envy Companies.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a joint liquidator possesses the legal standing to challenge antecedent transactions and recover assets from a fraudulent investment scheme.
Party_Details.Rule: Joint and several liquidators act collectively to administer an insolvent estate and may bring proceedings to recover company property and challenge antecedent transactions under insolvency legislation.
Party_Details.Application: Tay Puay Cheng, appointed as a joint liquidator, participates in the investigation of the Envy Companies' transactions and the commencement of proceedings against individuals and entities receiving misappropriated funds before liquidation.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Joint and several liquidators act collectively to administer an insolvent estate. Their statutory mandate includes tracing misappropriated corporate assets and bringing legal proceedings to set aside antecedent transactions that defraud creditors or constitute unfair preferences, ensuring maximum recovery for the estate.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Toh Ai Ling

Facts.Text: On 16 August 2021, Toh Ai Ling was appointed as one of the liquidators of the Envy Companies.
Toh Ai Ling was appointed as one of the liquidators of the Envy Companies on 16 August 2021.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a joint liquidator shares the collective authority to pursue claims against recipients of funds dissipated by an insolvent company.
Party_Details.Rule: Joint and several liquidators share authority to manage the insolvent estate and to bring proceedings to recover assets and challenge transactions entered into before liquidation.
Party_Details.Application: As part of the liquidator team, Toh Ai Ling participates in the collective actions to investigate the Envy Companies' financial affairs and pursue claims against recipients of company funds to recover the dissipated assets.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The collective authority of joint liquidators enables the comprehensive management of an insolvent estate, authorizing the pursuit of claims against third parties to recover dissipated assets under statutory avoidance provisions and common law restitutionary principles.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Ng Yu Zhi

Facts.Text: On 2015-10-08, Ng Yu Zhi was appointed as a director of Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd.
On 2019-12-10, Ng Yu Zhi was appointed as a director of Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.
From 2015-01-01 onwards, Ng Yu Zhi held at least 80% to 90% of the ordinary shares in the Envy Companies.

On 2016-08-29, Ng Yu Zhi asked an employee to prepare invoices to China MinMetals Corporation for earlier nickel and aluminium deals that had been agreed verbally, stating that the documents were needed for inclusion in a prospectus.
In 2017, Ng Yu Zhi told a colleague that he had engaged Grant Thornton to audit Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd and later stated that the audit was delayed due to a restructuring at Grant Thornton.
In 2019, Ng Yu Zhi participated in email discussions with KPMG Services Pte Ltd and a colleague about engaging KPMG Services Pte Ltd to audit Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd.
On 2019-11-01, Ng Yu Zhi contacted Poseidon Nickel Limited to inquire about purchasing physical nickel.

In 2020-08-01, Ng Yu Zhi instructed preparation of Forward Contracts showing Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd selling nickel to Raffemet Pte Ltd and provided details such as tonnage, price, contract date, and expiry date.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a controlling director who orchestrates a fraudulent investment scheme, directs the fabrication of transaction documents, and diverts corporate funds incurs personal liability for the resulting insolvency.
Party_Details.Rule: Company controllers and directors who design and operate fraudulent investment schemes, direct the use of company funds without commercial justification, and instruct the creation of forged documents are personally liable under statutory insolvency provisions (such as fraudulent trading) and common law claims (such as unlawful means conspiracy and breach of fiduciary duty).
Party_Details.Application: Ng Yu Zhi founds and controls the Envy Companies, owning up to 90% of the shares. He directs the investment scheme by instructing the preparation of fake invoices to China MinMetals, ordering the creation of fraudulent Forward Contracts with Raffemet, and feeding false information regarding external audits. Furthermore, he directs the massive transfer of company funds to accounts under his control, leaving the companies insolvent and indebted to defrauded investors.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: A controlling director who designs and orchestrates a massive fraudulent investment scheme, directs the fabrication of trading documents, and diverts corporate funds to personal accounts breaches the fundamental fiduciary duties of honesty and loyalty. Such conduct constitutes fraudulent trading, unlawful means conspiracy, and a breach of trust. Consequently, the controlling director is personally liable to account for the dissipated assets and make restitution to the insolvent estate for the funds misappropriated.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Lee Si Ye

Facts.Text: Before joining the Envy Companies, Lee Si Ye was trained as an accountant and had previously worked as an auditor.
Around 2015, Lee Si Ye came to know Ng Yu Zhi during her past employment as an auditor.
On 2015-11-12, Lee Si Ye was appointed as a director of Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd.
On 2020-04-13, Lee Si Ye was appointed as a director of Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.
On 2020-06-18, Lee Si Ye was appointed as a director of Envy Management Holdings Pte Ltd.
From 2015-01-01 onwards, Lee Si Ye held approximately 10% to 20% of the ordinary shares in the Envy Companies.

From 2015-11-12 onwards, in her role at the Envy Companies, Lee Si Ye was responsible for back-office functions including preparation of audit schedules, cash flow statements, balance sheets, profit and loss statements, management accounts, and unaudited financial statements based on information provided to her.
From 2015-11-12 onwards, Lee Si Ye provided template agreements to be used as Letters of Agreement for investors in the investment scheme.

From 2015-11-12 onwards, Lee Si Ye maintained and oversaw Excel-based investment trackers that recorded inflows and outflows between the Envy Companies and investors, initially populating them herself and later supervising support staff who updated them.
From 2015-11-12 onwards, Lee Si Ye calculated commissions, salaries, bonuses, and profit-sharing amounts to be paid to employees of the Envy Companies.
Before 2017-01-01, Lee Si Ye personally sent monthly market outlook updates and investment reports to investors of the Envy Companies.
From 2017-01-01 onwards, when the client management team was short-handed, Lee Si Ye occasionally assisted in sending monthly updates and reports to investors and was copied on such communications.

From 2016-01-01 onwards, Lee Si Ye was one of two authorised signatories, together with Ng Yu Zhi, for various bank accounts owned by the Envy Companies, with transactions above S$200,000 requiring two signatures.
In 2016-09-01, Lee Si Ye informed Ng Yu Zhi that the financial records of the Envy Companies were incomplete and that records for multiple periods were missing.
In 2017, Lee Si Ye asked Ng Yu Zhi about arranging an external audit of Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd.

In 2019, Lee Si Ye contacted KPMG Services Pte Ltd to explore engaging them as external auditors for Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd and participated in email discussions with KPMG Services Pte Ltd and Ng Yu Zhi about this engagement.

In 2016, Lee Si Ye prepared or contributed to the preparation of a prospectus for Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd that stated the company was licensed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore and categorised as a Registered Fund Management Company.

On 2016-10-06, Lee Si Ye emailed an investor describing nickel and aluminium deals involving Poseidon Nickel Limited, Meikles EC, and China MinMetals Corporation, including purchase and delivery dates.

On 2016-08-29, after receiving a message from Ng Yu Zhi stating that previous nickel and aluminium deals had been agreed verbally and that documents were needed for a prospectus, Lee Si Ye assisted in preparing invoices addressed to China MinMetals Corporation.
From 2018-01-01 onwards, Lee Si Ye received emails from staff highlighting discrepancies and inaccuracies in the Envy Companies' financial records.

From 2018-01-01 onwards, Lee Si Ye was copied on monthly investor statements and market outlook reports sent to investors, which included personalised spreadsheets of each investor's investment performance.
From 2018-01-01 onwards, Lee Si Ye communicated with Ng Yu Zhi about the state of the Envy Companies' financial records and the need for external audits.

On 2020-09-16, Lee Si Ye emailed an investor stating that BNP was still the end buyer for nickel but that purchases were being made through a broker, Raffemet Pte Ltd, which she described as an established firm with more than 25 years of experience and a market value of about S$1 billion.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director and back-office manager who prepares financial records, assists in drafting invoices, and co-signs bank transfers based on information from the controlling director possesses the requisite knowledge or dishonesty to be liable for the underlying fraudulent investment scheme.
Party_Details.Rule: Liability for fraudulent trading, knowing receipt, and dishonest assistance requires proof of actual knowledge, willful blindness, or dishonesty regarding the underlying fraud. Negligence or a failure to independently verify the authenticity of transactions directed by a principal controller is insufficient to establish accessorial liability for the fraud.
Party_Details.Application: As a director and back-office manager holding up to 20% of the shares, Lee Si Ye prepares financial documents, maintains investment trackers, communicates with investors, assists in preparing invoices, and co-signs bank transactions. She relies on the information provided by Ng Yu Zhi. However, she actively questions him regarding missing financial records and pushes for external audits by firms such as Grant Thornton and KPMG. Her administrative actions facilitate the scheme, but they are performed under the direction of the controlling shareholder.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Liability for fraudulent trading, knowing receipt, and dishonest assistance requires a demonstration of actual knowledge, willful blindness, or dishonesty regarding the underlying fraud. A back-office manager who prepares documentation and oversees administrative functions based on information provided by the controlling director, without actual knowledge or deliberate avoidance of the truth regarding the scheme's fraudulent nature, does not possess the requisite mental state for accessorial liability. Mere negligence or the failure to independently verify the authenticity of transactions directed by the principal fraudster is insufficient to establish liability for the fraudulent scheme.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Ju Xiao

Facts.Text: Before joining the Envy Companies, Ju Xiao worked as a treasury and finance manager and as a trading manager for a company that traded physical metals including copper and nickel.
On 2019-12-10, Ju Xiao was appointed as a director of Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.
On 2019-12-02, Ju Xiao became Head of Trading for the Envy Companies.

From 2019-12-02 onwards, Ju Xiao conducted proprietary trading in financial products such as foreign exchange contracts for differences, options, and fixed-income bonds for Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd.

In 2020-08-01, Ju Xiao arranged the purchase of approximately 2969.26 metric tonnes of primary nickel briquettes from Raffemet Pte Ltd for Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd as part of the Singapore Nickel Shipment.

In 2020-08-01, following instructions from Ng Yu Zhi, Ju Xiao prepared Forward Contracts showing Envy Global Trading Pte Ltd selling nickel to Raffemet Pte Ltd and included details such as tonnage, sale price, contract date, and expiry date as provided to him.

In 2020-08-01, Ju Xiao communicated with staff of Envysion entities regarding the provision of shipping documents, payment invoices, and hedging transaction records for 2020 for due diligence purposes.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director and Head of Trading who executes proprietary trades, arranges a physical nickel shipment, and prepares forward contracts incurs personal liability for assisting in a fraudulent investment scheme.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors and trading heads who actively execute trades, prepare transaction documents, and interact with related investment entities owe fiduciary duties and must not assist in unlawful conduct. Senior executives with industry expertise who participate in transactions lacking genuine commercial substance may be liable for dishonest assistance or unlawful means conspiracy if they possess actual knowledge or are willfully blind to the fraud.
Party_Details.Application: As director and Head of Trading, Ju Xiao conducts proprietary trading, arranges the Singapore Nickel Shipment, prepares Forward Contracts with Raffemet Pte Ltd, and interacts with Envysion staff regarding documentation. With a background in physical metal trading, he executes these tasks under the direction of the controlling shareholder. These actions facilitate the production of documents used to portray a legitimate trading operation to investors and auditors.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: A director and Head of Trading who actively participates in structuring transactions, executing trades, and preparing the forward contracts that facilitate a fraudulent investment scheme breaches their fiduciary duties. When a senior executive utilizes corporate resources to execute transactions that lack genuine commercial substance and are designed to perpetuate a fraud on investors, they cross the threshold into dishonest assistance and unlawful means conspiracy. Such involvement renders the executive personally liable to the insolvent estate for the losses generated by the scheme.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Cheong Ming Feng

Facts.Text: On 2017-05-16, Cheong Ming Feng commenced employment with the Envy Companies as a Business Administrative Executive and Client Support Associate.
From 2017-05-16 onwards, in his role at the Envy Companies, Cheong Ming Feng reported to Ng Yu Zhi, Lee Si Ye, and another employee, Shen Xuhuai.

From 2017-05-16 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng filled in investors' personal details and investment amounts into pre-prepared Letters of Agreement and Receivables Purchase Agreements and sent these documents to investors by email for execution.
From 2017-05-16 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng sent prospectuses and fact sheets about the Envy Companies to potential and existing investors.
From 2017-05-16 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng prepared monthly statements for investors and responded to investor queries based on figures obtained from the investment trackers.
From 2017-05-16 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng assisted in arranging the sale of physical nickel when required.

From 2018-01-01 onwards, on instructions from Ng Yu Zhi, Cheong Ming Feng conveyed instructions to a third party, Heng Yong Hui, to alter or create documents including internet banking screenshots, trading statements, and shipping documents.

From 2018-01-01 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng forwarded sample letters of credit, marine cargo insurance policies, and bills of lading to Heng Yong Hui and instructed him to edit them according to instructions received from Ng Yu Zhi.

On 2018-10-01, Cheong Ming Feng sent a United Overseas Bank internet banking screenshot to Heng Yong Hui and instructed him to change the sender name and amount to reflect a large inward remittance from BNP Paribas Commodity Futures Ltd.

From 2018-01-01 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng instructed Heng Yong Hui to alter screenshots of Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd's United Overseas Bank account balances to show higher balances than the actual balances.

From 2018-01-01 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng instructed Heng Yong Hui to alter sample trading statements in the name of BNP entities to create documents showing nickel trades for Envy Asset Management Pte Ltd.

From 2018-01-01 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng instructed Heng Yong Hui to alter sample shipping documents, including letters of credit and marine cargo insurance policies, to show Envy entities as parties to nickel shipments.
From 2017-01-01 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng received salary, allowances, bonuses, commissions, profit sharing, and other payments from the Envy Companies for his work.
From 2017-01-01 onwards, Cheong Ming Feng invested approximately S$3,000,000 into the Purported Nickel Trading and, by the time of the dispute, had withdrawn less than his total investment.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether an administrative employee who conveys instructions from a superior to alter bank and shipping documents, and acts as a liaison with investors, possesses the requisite knowledge or dishonesty to be held liable as an accessory to a fraudulent scheme.
Party_Details.Rule: Employees who act as mere administrative conduits for instructions from a controlling director are not liable for dishonest assistance or unlawful means conspiracy unless it is proven that they possessed actual knowledge of, or were willfully blind to, the fraudulent nature of the underlying scheme.
Party_Details.Application: As an administrative and client support employee, Cheong Ming Feng prepares and sends investment documents to investors and conveys instructions from the controller, Ng Yu Zhi, to a third party to alter banking, trading, and shipping documents. He also personally invests S$3 million of his own funds into the nickel trading scheme, ultimately losing money on these investments. His role is strictly logistical, reporting directly to senior management.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: An administrative employee who performs routine logistical tasks and acts as a mere conduit for instructions from a controlling director lacks the requisite knowledge and control to be held liable for the underlying corporate fraud. If the employee's actions—such as conveying instructions to alter documents—are performed without an understanding of the overarching fraudulent scheme and without dishonest intent, accessorial liability for dishonest assistance or unlawful means conspiracy is not established. An employee's substantial personal investment into the very scheme they are accused of orchestrating strongly negates any inference of actual knowledge of the fraud. Without proof of actual knowledge or willful blindness, an administrative employee is not personally liable for the dissipated funds.
